text: 2-Iodo-3-hydroxypyridine (1I), chemical formula C5H4INO, molecular weight 220.99, is a single-component organic molecular crystal. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group Pna21 (No. 33), with unit cell a = 11.5329 Å, b = 12.8331 Å, c = 4.2475 Å, Z = 4. Molecules are connected by O-H...N hydrogen bonds and halogen-mediated Br...Br interactions, with a crystal density of 2.335 g/cm³ determined at 100 K. The structure was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction with an R factor of 1.72% (CCDC 279639, CSD refcode YELQUC). This compound exhibits significant piezoelectric response, with predicted shear piezoelectricity d15 = 40.17 pC/N and longitudinal piezoelectricity d33 = 7.33 pC/N. Experimental measurements via piezoresponse force microscopy confirmed effective shear constants of 54 pC/N and longitudinal d33 values of approximately 6.7 pC/N. The crystal demonstrates high voltage constant g15 = 985.32 mV m/N for shear applications.
property_ref_title: Halogen and hydrogen bonded 2-X-pyridin-3-ol (X = Cl, Br, I) organic crystals with large shear piezoelectricity
